clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Increase detection, diagnosis, and specific targeted therapy for primary aldosteronism in hypertensive patients
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Hypertension evaluation, resistant hypertension workup, adrenal mass incidental finding, young stroke evaluation
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Screening eligibility determination + biochemical testing + specialist referral

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for primary aldosteronism screening
Identify qualifying conditions (resistant HTN, hypokalemia, OSA, adrenal mass, family history, young stroke)
Determine if COR 1 or COR 2b criteria met
Order plasma aldosterone, renin activity, ARR ratio
Refer to hypertension specialist or endocrinologist if positive or continued suspicion
Log screening results and referral status

validationRules: At least one qualifying condition for COR 1 screening
Stage 2 hypertension alone qualifies for COR 2b
Continue most antihypertensives except MRAs prior to screening

rules: IF (resistant HTN OR hypokalemia OR OSA OR adrenal mass OR family history early-onset HTN OR young stroke <40 years) AND hypertension present → COR 1, LOE C-EO → Recommend screening
ELSE IF stage 2 hypertension present → COR 2b, LOE C-EO → May consider screening
IF screening indicated → Order plasma aldosterone, renin activity, ARR ratio (COR 1, LOE C-LD)
IF screening indicated → Continue most antihypertensives except MRAs (COR 1, LOE C-EO)
IF positive screening OR suppressed renin OR disproportionate target organ damage → Refer to specialist (COR 1, LOE C-EO)

recommendations.condition: Hypertension + (resistant HTN OR hypokalemia OR OSA OR adrenal mass OR family history early-onset HTN OR young stroke <40 years)
recommendations.goal: Increase detection, diagnosis, and targeted therapy
Increase detection, diagnosis, and targeted therapy

recommendations.action: Use plasma aldosterone, renin activity, ARR ratio for initial screening
recommendations.goal: Assess biochemical evidence of primary aldosteronism

recommendations.action: Continue most antihypertensives (except MRAs) prior to screening
recommendations.goal: Minimize barriers to or delays in screening

recommendations.condition: Hypertension + positive screening OR continued suspicion (suppressed renin OR disproportionate target organ damage)
recommendations.action: Refer to hypertension specialist or endocrinologist

qualifyingConditions.description: List of conditions that triggered recommendation

appDisplay.behavior: Normal routing to screening or referral

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Follow COR 1 or COR 2b screening recommendation based on patient profile
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Complete screening within 4-6 weeks of indication identification
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Repeat ARR if initial test indeterminate or if clinical status changes
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag positive screening for specialist referral within 2 weeks
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log qualifying conditions, screening results, ARR ratio, and referral status
